summary: The diff implements most of the requested requirements: backend extended with customerPrincipal on delivery records, feedback system (submitFeedback, getFlaggedFeedback), resolveFeedback admin function, customer portal at /portal route with II login, product catalog, delivery history, feedback form, and FlaggedFeedbackSection in admin dashboard. However, the route is /portal instead of the required /customer. The ResolveFeedbackDialog does not expose a 'select new delivery status' option — it simply marks feedback resolved without allowing admin to pick a newDeliveryStatus. REQ-3 AC specifically requires the delivery status to be updated to a new value chosen by the admin.

missing_requirements.requirement: Customer portal must be accessible at the /customer route
missing_requirements.reason: The route is registered as /portal in App.tsx, not /customer as specified in the requirement.

missing_requirements.requirement: resolveFeedback(feedbackId, newDeliveryStatus) must allow admin to specify a new delivery status, and the delivery status must be updated to that value
missing_requirements.reason: The ResolveFeedbackDialog does not present a status selection field; it calls resolveFeedback with only feedbackId. The frontend mutation only passes feedbackId, suggesting the backend function may not accept or apply a newDeliveryStatus parameter as required.

missing_requirements.requirement: Admin can select a new status and resolve the feedback via a form/dialog
missing_requirements.reason: ResolveFeedbackDialog has no status selector input — the admin cannot choose a new delivery status before resolving, contradicting the AC that requires selecting a new status.

hallucinated_features.description: ProfileSetupModal for new customer name entry on first login
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request non-goals explicitly exclude any customer registration flow beyond Internet Identity. A profile setup modal prompting for a name is an additional registration step not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: backend/migration.mo module providing explicit data migration logic from legacy delivery records
hallucinated_features.reason: No migration module was requested in the build request. The requirements only asked to add a customerPrincipal field to delivery records, not to provide a formal migration module.
